moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the Lasker Simul Special, an offbeat opening where we fianchetto our kingside bishop to control the long diagonal and build a flexible position. This move prepares to develop our bishop to g2, where it will eye the entire a1-h8 diagonal and support our center from a distance. Rather than fighting for central space immediately like in 1.e4 or 1.d4, we opt for a patient, solid setup that's full of positional ideas—Lasker himself loved this approach in simultaneous exhibitions because it gives Black subtle problems without obvious complications.

Black immediately counterattacks on the kingside, moving the h-pawn two squares to create space and potentially challenge your fianchettoed bishop. This is an aggressive response that gains space and seizes the initiative before you can fully establish your setup; Black wants to prevent you from consolidating your position too comfortably. You'll need to decide whether to support your center, expand in the middle of the board, or address Black's kingside ambitions directly.
